title: ITR-4 Eligible — Presumptive Income under Section 44AD or 44ADA
description: A Resident individual or HUF (not a company or LLP) whose total income does not exceed ₹50 lakh and who has income from business computed under Section 44AD (turnover ≤ ₹3 crore, cash receipts ≤ 5%) or profession under Section 44ADA (receipts ≤ ₹75 lakh) is eligible to file ITR-4, provided no disqualifying conditions apply.

logic.if: taxpayer.resident_status == 'ROR' AND (taxpayer.income.presumptive_44AD == true OR taxpayer.income.presumptive_44ADA == true) AND taxpayer.income.total <= 5000000 AND taxpayer.is_director == false AND taxpayer.holds_unlisted_shares == false AND taxpayer.has_foreign_assets == false AND taxpayer.income.capital_gains == false

authority.section: Section 44AD and Section 44ADA read with ITR-4 Instructions AY2025-26
authority.proviso: Proviso to Section 44AD(4): If income declared lower than presumptive, books must be maintained and audit is required.

notes: Finance Act 2023 increased the 44AD threshold from ₹2 Cr to ₹3 Cr (with condition that cash receipts ≤ 5%). The 44ADA threshold was also increased from ₹50 L to ₹75 L. These limits apply from AY2024-25 onwards.
